name: Nagano Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: warmth, vitality and the rising sun over the Japanese Alps
purity and the snow of the surrounding mountains

description: An orange field charged at the centre with a white circular emblem containing a stylised abstraction of the katakana ナ.
history: The prefectural symbol was officially adopted on 12 September 1966, displayed in white on an orange field.

features.symbolism: a stylised abstraction of the katakana ナ (na) of Nagano enclosed within a circle, evoking unity and harmony among the prefecture's people.
